explanation: This command simulates a typical Netcat listener configuration that would trigger the Sigma rule. It uses the standard syntax for launching a listening session on port 4444.
This command is a variation using 'ncat.exe' and reorders the flags while maintaining the same functionality as the original command. It should also trigger the Sigma rule.
This command uses 'netcat.exe' instead of 'nc.exe' or 'ncat.exe' and maintains the same command-line structure. It is a valid alternative that should trigger the Sigma rule.
This command adds an additional flag to execute a shell, which is a common malicious use case for Netcat. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '--sh-exec' parameter.
This command uses the '-e' flag to execute a command shell, which is a common method for establishing a reverse shell. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '-e' parameter.
This command combines multiple flags to create a verbose listener and execute a command shell. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '-vnl' and '-e' parameters.

This command uses the '--exec' flag to execute a command shell, which is a common method for establishing a reverse shell. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '--exec' parameter.
This command uses a reordering of the flags while maintaining the same functionality. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '-e' parameter.
This command uses a reordering of the flags and includes the '--sh-exec' parameter to execute a shell. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '--sh-exec' parameter.
This command combines multiple flags to create a verbose listener and execute a command shell. It should trigger the Sigma rule due to the inclusion of the '-vnl' and '-e' parameters.
